statement.markdown: Given a non-empty string s, you need to check if it can be constructed from the letters of another string that has each letter in at most one in a row. The other string must be a palindrome.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be a string s.
statement.outputFormat: Output will be a boolean value indicating whether s can be constructed from the other string.
statement.notes: Time and space complexity are expected to be O(n).

starterCode.java: public class Solution {public boolean solve(String s);};
starterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, s: str) -> bool:

buggyStarterCode.cpp: bool Solution::solve(string s) {unordered_map<char, int> mp; for(int i=0; i<s.length(); i++) {if(mp[s[i]]) return false; mp[s[i]]=1;} return true;}
buggyStarterCode.java: public boolean Solution.solve(String s) {HashSet<Character> set = new HashSet<>(); for(int i=0; i<s.length(); i++) {if(set.contains(s.charAt(i))) return false; set.add(s.charAt(i));} return true;}
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, s: str) -> bool: count=0; for i in range(len(s)): if s[i] in self.mp: return False; self.mp[s[i]]=1; return True;
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function solve(s) {var map = new Map(); for(var i=0; i<s.length; i++) {if(map.has(s[i])) return false; map.set(s[i], 1);} return true;
